scenarios.opening_query: My teenage son's brand-new license got pulled and the letter's reasons make no sense to us.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Administrative Law > DMV (Non-Criminal) || Criminal Law > Juvenile Offender
scenarios.hidden_fact: The suspension is automatic fallout from a juvenile citation — minor-in-possession at a bonfire — and the juvenile case is where it gets fixed.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's automatic fallout from a juvenile minor-in-possession citation — the juvenile case is where it gets fixed.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a juvenile citation drives the suspension
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No citation anywhere — the DMV coded his vision-test waiver wrong, a pure records fix.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No citation — the DMV coded his vision waiver wrong.

scenarios.opening_query: I blew clean on the breathalyzer and they STILL took my license on the spot.
scenarios.hidden_fact: The officer claimed drug impairment — a DRE exam followed and I'm charged with DUII-controlled-substances despite the clean breath test; the criminal case is very live.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: They claimed drug impairment — DRE exam, and I'm charged with DUII-controlled-substances despite blowing clean.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a drug-based DUII charge was filed
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No charge issued — but the implied-consent suspension for the 'refused' urine test is running, and that hearing is the fight.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No charge — but the refusal suspension over the urine test is running, and that hearing's the fight.

scenarios.opening_query: A parking enforcement mix-up spiraled and now the city says I owe $3,000 and can't renew my registration.
scenarios.hidden_fact: The tickets are all from a city-owned lot's broken kiosk — dozens of us got them the same weeks, and the city's administrative-review process for the batch is the venue.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: They're all from the city lot's broken kiosk — dozens of us got them, and the city's own review process is the venue.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether municipal ticket review rather than DMV process governs
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The tickets are valid and paid — the DMV just never lifted its registration hold, a records failure on their end.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Paid and valid — the DMV just never lifted the hold.

scenarios.opening_query: My DUI diversion requires a treatment program my insurance suddenly stopped covering halfway through.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Criminal Law > DUII/DWS || General Litigation > Insurance (Health/Disability)
scenarios.hidden_fact: The diversion itself is on track and the court granted an extension — the live fight is the insurer's mid-treatment coverage cutoff, which their own policy language doesn't support.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Diversion's on track with a court extension — the fight is the insurer's mid-treatment cutoff, which their own policy doesn't support.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the coverage denial is the operative dispute
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The insurance dispute made me miss sessions — diversion revocation is on the calendar, and saving it is the emergency.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The missed sessions triggered a revocation setting — saving diversion is the emergency.

scenarios.opening_query: I loaned my truck to my cousin and now there's impound fees, tickets, and something about a court date in my name.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Administrative Law > DMV (Non-Criminal) || Criminal Law > Traffic Offenses
scenarios.hidden_fact: The court date is a criminal careless-driving citation issued to the registered owner — me — because he fled the stop; contesting and redirecting that citation is the legal work.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The court date is a careless-driving citation issued to me as owner because he fled the stop — contesting and redirecting it is the work.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether an owner-liability citation is criminal-court work
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No citation on me — it's the impound-release and registration-hold tangle at the DMV counter.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Nothing criminal on me — it's the impound and registration-hold tangle.

scenarios.opening_query: After my seizure diagnosis I self-reported like the law requires, and the DMV response has been chaos.
scenarios.final: Labor & Employment > ADA (Disability) Act Issues - Employee
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Administrative Law > DMV (Non-Criminal) || Labor & Employment > ADA (Disability) Act Issues - Employee
scenarios.hidden_fact: The DMV part will resolve — the emergency is my employer, who saw the paperwork and pulled me off ALL duties including desk work I can safely do; the accommodation fight is the case.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The DMV part will sort out — my employer saw the paperwork and pulled me off even desk work I can safely do. The accommodation fight is the case.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether employer overreaction is the operative harm
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Work's been great — the DMV itself lost my neurologist's clearance twice and suspended me anyway; the agency process is the fight.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Work's been supportive — the DMV lost my clearance twice and suspended me anyway.
scenarios.vetting_note: Employer-vs-agency locus after a medical report.
